title: Plotting the fit error band within the Combine tool

posts.name: Xuli Yan
posts.text:
Hello experts,
I am writing to see if there is a way to access the fitted error band within the combine tool, with combine -M FitDiagnostics? In the bare RooFit, one can fit the data, get the RooFitResult and plot the fitted model with VisualizeError(). However, combine -M FitDiagnostics only gives the fits as TH1F histograms and data as TGraphAsymmErrors. I am not very sure how to add the fitting error band on top of them…
Do you have some suggestions?
Thanks a lot!
Sebastian
posts.cooked_html:
Hello experts,

I am writing to see if there is a way to access the fitted error band within the combine tool, with combine -M FitDiagnostics? In the bare RooFit, one can fit the data, get the RooFitResult and plot the fitted model with VisualizeError(). However, combine -M FitDiagnostics only gives the fits as TH1F histograms and data as TGraphAsymmErrors. I am not very sure how to add the fitting error band on top of them…

Do you have some suggestions?

Thanks a lot!
Sebastian

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
–saveWithUncertainties
--saveWithUncertainties Save also pre/post-fit uncertainties on
the shapes and normalizations (from
resampling the covariance matrix)

-Andrea
On Wed, Mar 23, 2022 at 11:05 PM Xuli Yan via CMS talk <noreply@cms-talk.web.cern.ch> wrote:

posts.cooked_html:
–saveWithUncertainties

--saveWithUncertainties Save also pre/post-fit uncertainties on
the shapes and normalizations (from
resampling the covariance matrix)

-Andrea

posts.name: Xuli Yan
posts.text:
Hi Andrea,
Thanks for your quick reply!
Okay, I tried --saveWithUncertainties and it worked, but I am wondering how to get the errors corresponding to 2 sigma? I guess the error associated now is only 1 sigma, right?
Thanks!
Best regards,
Sebastian
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Andrea,

Thanks for your quick reply!

Okay, I tried --saveWithUncertainties and it worked, but I am wondering how to get the errors corresponding to 2 sigma? I guess the error associated now is only 1 sigma, right?

Thanks!

Best regards,
Sebastian
